meta.title: Riemann Hypothesis via IOGT: Conditional Proof
meta.subtitle: Operational Attractor Framework
meta.author: Zelenka_D_D

citations.cite_as: Zelenka, D. D. (2026). The Riemann Hypothesis as an Operational Attractor Problem: A Conditional Analysis via IOGT. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18190384

abstract: We prove the Riemann Hypothesis conditionally on the validity of the Intrinsic Operational Gradient Theorem (IOGT). Key insight: nontrivial zeros are stable objects in the operational landscape of prime threading. Self-adjointness forces ѕГ=1/2 as the unique gradient minimum in the critical strip. Off-line zeros would require unbounded reconstruction cost, violating IOGT stability conditions. Result: IOGT вЯє RH.

core_claim.conditional_theorem: IF IOGT (operations-primary ontology) is valid, THEN all nontrivial zeros of ќґ(s) satisfy Re(s) = 1/2
core_claim.interpretation: If operations are ontologically primary, RH follows as structural necessity

P3.name: Adjoint_Relation
P3.significance: breaks naive self-adjointness, key to ѕГ=1/2

P4.name: Test_Space
P4.definition: Schwartz functions with exponential decay in Fourier space

P5.name: Renormalized_Prime_Operator
P5.definition: рЭТЃћГ_ѕГ ѕЖ(t) = ќ£_p p^(-ѕГ)[ѕЖ(t+log p) + ѕЖ(t-log p)] - вИЂ_2^вИЮ x^(-ѕГ)[ѕЖ(t+log x) + ѕЖ(t-log x)] dx/log x

A1.name: IOGT_Foundation
A1.statement: In composable operational systems with non-invertibility, stable objects occupy local minima of operational potential V(x) where вИЗV = 0
A1.ontological_claim: operations are primary, objects are secondary

A2.name: Zeros_Are_Stable
A2.statement: Nontrivial zeros of ќґ(s) are stable objects

evidence.theoretical_invariance: preserved by functional equation ќґ(s) = ќґ(1-sћД)

A2.implication: zeros must satisfy stability conditions from A1

A3.name: Prime_Threading_Satisfies_IOGT
A3.statement: The system (рЭТЯ(рЭТЃ), {S_{log p}}_{p prime}, вАЦ¬ЈвАЦ_вДЛ) satisfies IOGT conditions

conditions.composability: S_{log p} вИШ S_{log q} = S_{log(pq)}
conditions.non_invertibility: renormalized sum ќ£_p - вИЂ collapses вИЮ operational histories

A3.verification: follows from operator algebra and renormalization structure

L1.name: Self_Adjointness_Forces_Critical_Line
L1.statement: рЭТЃћГ_ѕГ* = рЭТЃћГ_{1-ѕГ}. Self-adjointness requires рЭТЃћГ_ѕГ = рЭТЃћГ_ѕГ* вЯє ѕГ = 1-ѕГ вЯє ѕГ = 1/2
L1.proof_sketch: By P3, adjoint operation flips ѕГ to 1-ѕГ. Self-adjointness (рЭТЃћГ_ѕГ = рЭТЃћГ_ѕГ*) forces ѕГ = 1-ѕГ

L2.name: Critical_Line_Is_Unique_Gradient_Minimum
L2.statement: ѕГ = 1/2 is unique critical point of operational potential V(ѕГ,ќ≥) = |ќї(ѕГ,ќ≥)| in critical strip

proof_steps.step_1: Functional equation symmetry about ѕГ=1/2 вЯє вИВV/вИВѕГ|_{ѕГ=1/2} = 0
proof_steps.step_2: For ѕГ вЙ† 1/2: weight mismatch p^(-ѕГ) вЙ† p^(-(1-ѕГ)) вЯє вИВV/вИВѕГ вЙ† 0

L2.geometric_interpretation: critical line is saddle point in operational landscape

L3.name: Threading_Coherence_Only_At_Critical_Line
L3.statement: Discrete-continuous cancellation (ќї=0) requires weight calibration at ѕГ=1/2
L3.eigenvalue_formula: ќї(ќ≥,ѕГ) = 2[ќ£_p p^(-ѕГ)cos(ќ≥ log p) - вИЂ_2^вИЮ x^(-ѕГ)cos(ќ≥ log x) dx/log x]

analysis.prime_number_theorem: governs asymptotic behavior of discrete sum
analysis.mismatch_growth: For ѕГ вЙ† 1/2: ќФ(ѕГ,N) = |ќ£_{pвЙ§N} p^(-ѕГ) - вИЂ_2^N x^(-ѕГ) dx/log x| вЖТ вИЮ

L4.name: Off_Line_Reconstruction_Cost_Unbounded
L4.statement: Zeros off critical line violate IOGT finite potential requirement

mechanism.reason: Must distinguish among 2^(ѕА(N)) possible resonance patterns with wrong weights
mechanism.ratio: C_rev/C_fwd вЖТ вИЮ as N вЖТ вИЮ
mechanism.violation: unbounded cost ratio violates IOGT finite potential requirement

L5.name: Spectral_Correspondence
L5.statement: Spectrum of рЭТЃ (where ќї(ќ≥)=0) вЯЇ imaginary parts of zeros ѕБ = 1/2 + iќ≥ of ќґ(s)
L5.mechanism: Weil explicit formula: Fourier transform of operator action = sum over zeros

main_theorem.name: Riemann_Hypothesis_Conditional
main_theorem.statement: All nontrivial zeros of ќґ(s) satisfy Re(s) = 1/2

proof_steps.claim: Prime threading system satisfies IOGT conditions
вИі Zeros must lie at gradient minima
ѕГ = 1/2 is unique gradient minimum in critical strip
Confirmation: Threading coherence (ќї=0) only at ѕГ=1/2
Exclusion: Off-line zeros violate IOGT (unbounded reconstruction cost)
вИі All nontrivial zeros satisfy Re(s) = 1/2

why_sigma_half.question: Why ѕГ = 1/2?
why_sigma_half.answer: Self-adjointness forces it as unique balance point between forward/reverse threading operations

why_not_off_line.question: Why not ѕГ вЙ† 1/2?
why_not_off_line.answer: Weight mismatch creates unbounded operational cost, violating attractor stability requirements

paradigm_shift.advantage: converts existence problem to admissibility problem

conditionality.interpretation: If operations are ontologically primary (as IOGT claims), then RH follows as structural necessity
conditionality.unconditional_path: requires independent validation of IOGT axioms

cost_falsification.method: Showing bounded reconstruction cost for ѕГвЙ†1/2

applicability_falsification.method: Demonstrating IOGT doesn't apply to prime threading

ќґ(s).name: Riemann zeta function
ќґ(s).definition: ќґ(s) = ќ£_{n=1}^вИЮ n^(-s) for Re(s) > 1, analytically continued

one_sentence_summary: If stable mathematical objects must occupy operational gradient minima (IOGT), then self-adjointness forces the critical line ѕГ=1/2 as the unique such minimum for prime threading, making it the only location where zeros can exist.

full_proof_recovery: 1. Construct Hilbert space вДЛ = L¬≤(вДЭ, e^(-t)dt) with weighted inner product
2. Define shift operators S_a and compute adjoint S_a* = e^a S_{-a}
3. Build renormalized prime operator рЭТЃћГ_ѕГ with discrete-continuous structure
4. Verify IOGT conditions: composability, bounded forward cost, non-invertibility
5. Prove self-adjointness relation рЭТЃћГ_ѕГ* = рЭТЃћГ_{1-ѕГ} using P3
7. Analyze operational potential V(ѕГ,ќ≥) via functional equation symmetry
8. Prove ѕГ = 1/2 is unique critical point (Lemma L2)
9. Compute eigenvalue ќї(ќ≥,ѕГ) and analyze cancellation conditions
10. Show threading coherence requires ѕГ = 1/2 (Lemma L3)
11. Calculate forward/reverse costs, prove exponential gap for ѕГвЙ†1/2 (Lemma L4)
12. Establish spectral correspondence via Weil explicit formula (Lemma L5)
13. Apply IOGT: stable zeros вЖТ gradient minima вЖТ ѕГ = 1/2
verification_checklist: Verify weighted inner product computation for adjoint
Validate cost estimates (forward polynomial, reverse exponential)
Verify Weil formula connection to operator spectrum
Check IOGT axioms apply to this system

computational_metadata.numerical_validation: zeros can be computed and verified to lie on critical line to arbitrary precision
